existed from 1559[[1]](#cite_note-1) until the [French Revolution](/wiki/French_Revolution "French Revolution"). Its see at [Saint-Omer](/wiki/Saint-Omer "Saint-Omer"), in the modern department of [Pas-de-Calais](/wiki/Pas-de-Calais "Pas-de-Calais"), was created as a reaction to the destruction of the [see of Thérouanne](/wiki/See_of_Th%C3%A9rouanne "See of Thérouanne"), by military action in the wars of the [Emperor Charles V](/wiki/Emperor_Charles_V "Emperor Charles V"). It then became a suffragan of the [Archdiocese of Cambrai](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Archdiocese_of_Cambrai "Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Cambrai") in 1559.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Roman Catholic diocese in France (1559 - 1801)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Ancient Diocese of Saint-Omer"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Ancient+Diocese+of+Saint-Omer%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Ancient+Diocese+of+Saint-Omer%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Ancient+Diocese+of+Saint-Omer%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Ancient+Diocese+of+Saint-Omer%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Ancient+Diocese+of+Saint-Omer%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Ancient+Diocese+of+Saint-Omer%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(December 2016)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fb/Cath%C3%A9drale_Notre-Dame_de_Saint-Omer1.JPG/250px-Cath%C3%A9drale_Notre-Dame_de_Saint-Omer1.JPG)](/wiki/File:Cath%C3%A9drale_Notre-Dame_de_Saint-Omer1.JPG)

[Saint-Omer Cathedral](/wiki/Saint-Omer_Cathedral "Saint-Omer Cathedral")

The former French Catholic **diocese of Saint-Omer** existed from 1559[[1]](#cite_note-1) until the [French Revolution](/wiki/French_Revolution "French Revolution"). Its see at [Saint-Omer](/wiki/Saint-Omer "Saint-Omer"), in the modern department of [Pas-de-Calais](/wiki/Pas-de-Calais "Pas-de-Calais"), was created as a reaction to the destruction of the [see of Thérouanne](/wiki/See_of_Th%C3%A9rouanne "See of Thérouanne"), by military action in the wars of the [Emperor Charles V](/wiki/Emperor_Charles_V "Emperor Charles V"). It then became a suffragan of the [Archdiocese of Cambrai](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Archdiocese_of_Cambrai "Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Cambrai") in 1559.

By the [Concordat of 1801](/wiki/Concordat_of_1801 "Concordat of 1801"), the diocese of [Saint-Omer](/wiki/Saint-Omer "Saint-Omer") was united with the [diocese of Arras](/wiki/Diocese_of_Arras "Diocese of Arras") and the [diocese of Boulogne](/wiki/Diocese_of_Boulogne "Diocese of Boulogne"), to form an enlarged diocese of Arras.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Bishops

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ancient_Diocese_of_Saint-Omer&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Bishops")]

* [Guillaume de Poitiers](/wiki/Guillaume_de_Poitiers "Guillaume de Poitiers") 1561
* [Gérard de Haméricourt](/wiki/G%C3%A9rard_de_Ham%C3%A9ricourt "Gérard de Haméricourt") 1563-1577
* [Jean Six](/wiki/Jean_Six "Jean Six") 1581-1586
* [Jacques de Pamèle](/wiki/Jacques_de_Pam%C3%A8le "Jacques de Pamèle") 1587
* [Jean de Vernois](/w/index.php?title=Jean_de_Vernois&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jean de Vernois (page does not exist)"), O.P., 1591-1599
* [Jacques Blaseus](/wiki/Jacques_Blaseus "Jacques Blaseus"), [O.F.M. Rec.](/wiki/O.F.M._Rec. "O.F.M. Rec."), 1600-1618 (previously [bishop of Namur](/wiki/Bishop_of_Namur "Bishop of Namur"))
* [Paul Boudot](/wiki/Paul_Boudot "Paul Boudot") 1618-1626 (then [bishop of Arras](/wiki/Bishop_of_Arras "Bishop of Arras"))
* Pierre Paunet, O.F.M., 1628-1631
* Christophe de Morlet 1632-1633
* Christophe de France 1635-1656
* [Ladislas Jonart](/wiki/Ladislas_Jonart "Ladislas Jonart") 1662-1671 (then [archbishop of Cambrai](/wiki/Archbishop_of_Cambrai "Archbishop of Cambrai"))
* [Jacques-Théodore de Bryas](/wiki/Jacques-Th%C3%A9odore_de_Bryas "Jacques-Théodore de Bryas") 1672-1675 (then archbishop of Cambrai)
* Jean Charles de Longueval 1676
* Pierre Van Den Perre 1577
* Armand-Anne-Tristan de La Baume de Suze 1677
* Louis-Alphonse de Valbelle 1677-1708 (previously [bishop of Alet](/wiki/Bishop_of_Alet "Bishop of Alet"))
* François de Valbelle de Tourves 1708-1727
* Joseph-Alphonse de Valbelle de Tourves 1727-1754
* Pierre-Joseph de Brunes de Monlouet 1754-1765 (previously [bishop of Dol](/wiki/Bishop_of_Dol "Bishop of Dol"))
* Louis-François-Marc-Hilaire de Conzié 1766-1769 (then [bishop of Aire](/wiki/Bishop_of_Aire "Bishop of Aire"))
* Joachim-François-Mamert de Conzié 1769-1775 (then [archbishop of Tours](/wiki/Archbishop_of_Tours "Archbishop of Tours"))
* Jean-Auguste de Chastenet de Puységur 1775-1778 (then [bishop of Carcassonne](/wiki/Bishop_of_Carcassonne "Bishop of Carcassonne"))
* Alexandre-Joseph-Alexis de Bruyère de Chalabre 1778-1790 (1796)
